rationale.R1.6: The delegation asks only to look at a paragraph, making sentence- and paragraph-level readability, flow, and polish the central expected responsibility.
rationale.RX.1: Any assessment of the paragraph should disclose uncertainty because the request gives no specific review criterion or external evidence base.
rationale.RX.2: The projection should guard against endorsing broad firstness, reliability, or performance claims beyond what the paragraph itself establishes.
rationale.RX.3: The response must stay within the requested inspection of the paragraph and not expand into executing edits or a full paper review.
rationale.RX.4: Because the paragraph concerns scientific-computing reliability, the projection should preserve attention to harms from overstated accuracy or trustworthiness claims.
rationale.RX.5: Any responsibility should remain traceable to the text of the provided artifact rather than unstated assumptions about the underlying method or literature.
